LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARC PRESTON, SASHA SZABO, CHRISTOPHER APOLITO, AND COASTAL TRADE SECURITIES, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION ARE DEFICIENT BECAUSE, ALTHOUGH PARAGRAPH 8 OF THE COMPLAINT STATES THAT THE AGREEMENT SUED ON IN THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION IS ATTACHED TO THE COMPLAINT, THE FILED VERSION OF THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ATTACH ANY AGREEMENT. THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION LACK THE REQUIRED SPECIFICITY FOR THE PLEADING OF FRAUD CLAIMS. IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, PLAINTIFF VADIM RYABOY IS GIVEN LEAVE TO ALLEGE WELL-PLEAD COGNIZABLE CLAIMS AGAINST THE DEFENDANTS, OR SOME OF THEM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 =302/HK

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CRAIG JONES OF ARCHER NORRIS, TEL. NO. 1-925-930-6600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Marc Preston, Sasha Szabo, Christopher Apolito, and Coastal Trade Securities, LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to all causes of action alleged in the complaint is granted with twenty days leave to amend as to all causes of action. The first and second causes of action are deficient because, although paragraph 8 of the complaint states that the agreement sued on in those causes of action is attached to the complaint, the filed version of the complaint does not attach any agreement. The third and fourth causes of action lack the required specificity for the pleading of fraud claims. If he can do so in good faith, plaintiff Vadim Ryaboy is given leave to allege well-plead cognizable claims against the defendants, or some of them. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Feb-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JASUN C. MOLINELLI, ESQ. of Archer Norris APLC, Tel No. 925-930-6600 appearing for Defendants Marc Preston, Sasha Szabo, Christopher Apolito, and Coastal Trade Securities, LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter argued and submitted by defense counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MARC PRESTON, SASHA SZABO, CHRISTOPHER APOLITO, AND COASTAL TRADE SECURITIES, LLC'S Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens is denied. None of the relevant factors support granting the motion. Plaintiff is a California resident and his choice of forum is given substantial weight. California has a significant interest in providing its resident plaintiff with a forum for him to seek enforcement of his asserted contractual rights, especially since the contract was entered into in California. Nothing in the parties' contract requires that this case be in New York. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Mark Preston
Off Calendar Aug-06-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jasun C. Molinelli, of Archer Norris, Tel. 925.930.6600, for defendants;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MARC PRESTON, CHRISTOPHER APOLITO, COASTAL TRADE SECURITIES, LLC, SASHA SZABO'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons is granted in part. To effectuate substitute service: a copy of the summons and complaint must be left at the individual's dwelling house ("usual place of abode") with a "competent member of the household" or person "apparently in charge of his or her office, place of business, or usual mailing address," at least 18 years old, who must be told what the papers are. [See CCP 415.20(b).] The person with whom the papers are left need not be a member of the family. [See Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 CA4th 1387, 1393.] For example, a doorman in an apartment building is considered a "competent member of the household" where they control access to the residence. [Id. at 1393.] Here, Plainitff served Mr. Apolito at 32 Highland Drive, in New Jersey. This is not Defendant Apolito's residence, but is the residence of his mother. Defendant Apolito does not live at this address. Therefore the Court finds the substitute service to be invalid and grants the motion to quash as to Defendant Apolito. The proof of service filed with the court shows Defendant Szabo was filed via substitute service at his place of business at 40 Wall Street, 17th Floor, New York, New York. This is valid substitute service. Defendant argues Plaintiff served Defendant Szabo by leaving documents with his doorman, however this is also valid service under CA law. Therefore, the court denies the motion as to Defendant Szabo. The proof of service filed with the court shows Defendant Costal Trade Securities was filed via substitute service on Jessica Porter at its place of business 40 Wall Street, 17th Floor, New York, New York. This is valid substitute service. A second proof of service shows Mr. Preston was served at 425 Second Street STE 250. The court finds there has been valid service on agent for service of process Marc Preston for Costal Trade Securities, therefore the motion is denied as to Defendant Costal Trade Securities. Counsel shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
